changed_functions.name: VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE::`scalar deleting destructor'
key_changes: The inline destruction logic (list unlinking, pushlock acquire/release, operator delete) was extracted into a standalone ~VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE destructor at 0x1C000136C. The patched scalar deleting destructor was relocated to 0x1C000148C and reduced to 10 instructions that call ~VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE, then operator delete, then return.
changed_functions.security_notes: Function-extraction refactor. The destruction sequence is unchanged in effect; it was moved out of the deleting destructor into a dedicated ~VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE. Not tied to any behavioral security change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Unpatched scalar deleting destructor (0x1C000136C) contains inline destruction logic (81 instructions). Patched: ~VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE at 0x1C000136C holds that logic; the scalar deleting destructor moved to 0x1C000148C and is reduced to: push rbx; sub rsp, 0x20; mov rbx, rcx; call ~VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE; mov rcx, rbx; call operator delete; mov rax, rbx; add rsp, 0x20; pop rbx; retn.

changed_functions.name: VIDMM_GLOBAL::UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand
key_changes: Adds one VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE::ReleaseVaRangeReference call (0x1C0062EB7) on the AddVaRangeToVadRangeList failure path, gated behind Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() and taken only when AddVaRangeToVadRangeList returns a negative result. It releases the extra reference the caller adds under the same servicing flag.

The Feature_3895685435-guarded block that adds the VA range back to the VAD range list and then calls CleanupVaRangeReference (which drops one reference via ReleaseVaRangeReference) is present identically in BOTH builds; only the compensating release above is new.
Call targets shifted due to relocation (address offsets only, no semantic change to existing calls).
changed_functions.security_notes: Part of the same feature-staged refcount-balancing scheme as the caller change. The compensating release only executes when Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference is enabled. With the servicing flags disabled (default), this addition is inert and the function behaves as in the unpatched build.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Patched adds after the AddVaRangeToVadRangeList call (0x1C0062EA0) and before CleanupVaRangeReference (0x1C0062EBF):
  call    Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; 0x1C0062EA7
  test    eax, eax
  je      0x1C0062EBC
  test    esi, esi          ; esi = AddVaRangeToVadRangeList result
  jns     0x1C0062EBC        ; only on failure (< 0)
  mov     rcx, rbx
  call    ?ReleaseVaRangeReference@VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE@@QEAAJXZ  ; 0x1C0001338
The preceding Feature_3895685435 guard (0x1C0062E5A) and the managed-VAD flag test [rbx+0x40] & 0x2000 (0x1C0062E67) are identical to the unpatched function (0x1C0064536 / 0x1C0064543). Also changes 0x551F -> 0x552B for the WdLog assertion code.

changed_functions.name: VIDMM_GLOBAL::VidMmMapGpuVirtualAddressInternal
key_changes: In the uncommit branch (D3DDDI_MAPGPUVIRTUALADDRESS.Flags bit 3 set, i.e. arg4 & 8), the patched version inserts 'lock inc dword [rsi+0x80]' at 0x1C0066EDC BEFORE calling UncommitVirtualAddressRange. It is gated behind Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (0x1C0066ECA) AND Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (0x1C0066ED3). If either returns zero, control falls through to loc_1C0066EE3, which is identical to the unpatched no-increment path.
The commit branch (bit 3 clear) increments the refcount unconditionally in BOTH builds (unpatched 0x1C0066F34, patched 0x1C0066F0D).
Register reallocation: the object pointer is held in rbp in the unpatched build and rsi in the patched build; function relocated from 0x1C0066CB0 to 0x1C0066C70.
changed_functions.security_notes: The added increment balances a reference that the uncommit path can drop via UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand -> CleanupVaRangeReference -> ReleaseVaRangeReference. That reference-drop is itself guarded by Feature_3895685435 and the managed-VAD flag (0x2000) and is present identically in both builds. With the servicing flags disabled (default), the uncommit path drops no reference and the caller adds none, so patched and unpatched are behaviorally identical and no use-after-free is reachable. This is feature-staged refcount balancing, not a delivered, unconditionally active fix. The feature flags are internal servicing gates, not attacker-controllable input.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched uncommit branch (0x1C0066F10-0x1C0066F32):
  mov     rax, [rsp+0xd0]
  xor     r9d, r9d
  mov     r8, rbp            ; rbp = VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE*, no increment
  mov     [rsp+0x20], rax
  call    0x1C00643F0        ; UncommitVirtualAddressRange
  mov     r13, [rsp+0xc8]
  mov     edi, eax
  jmp     0x1C0066F90        ; join point

Patched uncommit branch:
  call    Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage   ; 0x1C0066ECA
  test    eax, eax
  je      0x1C0066EE3        ; flag off -> skip increment (== unpatched)
  call    Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; 0x1C0066ED3
  test    eax, eax
  je      0x1C0066EE3        ; flag off -> skip increment (== unpatched)
  lock inc dword ptr [rsi+0x80]  ; 0x1C0066EDC (staged increment)
  ; loc_1C0066EE3 (common continuation):
  mov     rax, [rsp+0xd0]
  xor     r9d, r9d
  mov     r8, rsi
  mov     [rsp+0x20], rax
  call    0x1C0062D14        ; UncommitVirtualAddressRange

Commit branch (both builds) increments unconditionally: unpatched 'lock inc dword [rbp+0x80]' at 0x1C0066F34, patched 'lock inc dword [rsi+0x80]' at 0x1C0066F0D.

security_findings.entry_point: D3DKMTMapGpuVirtualAddress / pfnMapGpuVirtualAddress (user-mode DirectX graphics API -> dxgkrnl.sys -> dxgmms2.sys)
call_chain: CleanupVaRangeReference (0x1C005D008) -> ReleaseVaRangeReference (0x1C0001338) [only under Feature_3895685435 + managed-VAD flag]
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Feature-staged reference-count balancing (CWE-416 lifetime class; not reachable in the delivered configuration)
security_findings.description: In VidMmMapGpuVirtualAddressInternal the mapping request is split by D3DDDI_MAPGPUVIRTUALADDRESS.Flags bit 3 (test byte [rdi+0x38], 8). The commit branch (bit clear) increments the VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE reference count at +0x80 unconditionally in both builds. The uncommit branch (bit set) has no such increment in the unpatched build; the patched build inserts 'lock inc dword [rsi+0x80]' at 0x1C0066EDC, but only when both Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() and Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() return nonzero. When either flag is disabled the patched code falls through to loc_1C0066EE3 and is identical to the unpatched path. The increment balances a reference that the uncommit path can drop inside UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand via CleanupVaRangeReference -> ReleaseVaRangeReference; that reference-drop block is guarded by Feature_3895685435 and the managed-VAD flag ([obj+0x40] & 0x2000) and is present identically in both builds. Therefore, in the default configuration (feature flags disabled) the uncommit path drops no reference, the caller adds none, and the patched and unpatched binaries are behaviorally identical with no lifetime imbalance. No attacker-reachable use-after-free exists in either binary as shipped. The feature flags are internal servicing gates, not attacker-controllable input.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: VidMmMapGpuVirtualAddressInternal is reachable from user mode via D3DKMTMapGpuVirtualAddress with D3DDDI_MAPGPUVIRTUALADDRESS.Flags bit 3 (0x8) set. Reaching the code that differs between the builds additionally requires the internal Feature_3895685435 feature to be enabled at runtime (so UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand executes the CleanupVaRangeReference reference-drop block) and the VA range to carry the managed-VAD flag [obj+0x40] & 0x2000. In the default configuration these preconditions are not met, so there is no divergence between the builds and no lifetime imbalance.
security_findings.primitive: None in the delivered binaries. The reference-count divergence is confined to feature-gated code paths that are inert unless internal servicing flags are enabled, with the pre-existing no-increment path retained as the default. In the shipped configuration the uncommit path does not drop a reference on the VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE object, so RecordVaPagingHistoryMapGpuVa and ReleaseVaRangeReferenceSafe operate on a live object. There is no demonstrable use-after-free, pool-reclaim window, or read/write primitive.
security_findings.evidence:
Unpatched VidMmMapGpuVirtualAddressInternal, uncommit branch (arg4 & 8) has no reference increment before the uncommit call:

  0x1C0066F10: mov  rax, [rsp+0xd0]
  0x1C0066F18: xor  r9d, r9d
  0x1C0066F1B: mov  r8, rbp                 ; VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE*, no increment
  0x1C0066F23: call 0x1C00643F0             ; UncommitVirtualAddressRange
  0x1C0066F32: jmp  0x1C0066F90             ; join point

Unpatched commit branch (arg4 & 8 clear) increments unconditionally:
  0x1C0066F34: lock inc dword [rbp+0x80]
  0x1C0066F64: call 0x1C00677B0             ; CommitVirtualAddressRange

Patched uncommit branch inserts the increment but gates it behind two feature flags, retaining the old path on the fall-through:
  0x1C0066ECA: call Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
  0x1C0066ED1: je   0x1C0066EE3             ; flag off -> skip (== unpatched)
  0x1C0066ED3: call Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
  0x1C0066EDA: je   0x1C0066EE3             ; flag off -> skip (== unpatched)
  0x1C0066EDC: lock inc dword [rsi+0x80]    ; staged increment
  0x1C0066EFC: call 0x1C0062D14             ; UncommitVirtualAddressRange

The reference-drop it balances is guarded identically in both builds inside UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand:
  (unpatched) 0x1C0064536: call Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
              0x1C0064543: test dword [rbx+0x40], 0x2000
              0x1C0064586: call CleanupVaRangeReference
  (patched)   0x1C0062E5A: call Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
              0x1C0062E67: test dword [rbx+0x40], 0x2000
              0x1C0062EBF: call CleanupVaRangeReference

CleanupVaRangeReference (0x1C005D008) calls ReleaseVaRangeReference (0x1C0001338), which decrements the refcount via 'lock xadd [rcx+0x80], -1' and invokes the deleting destructor only when the pre-decrement value was 1.
security_findings.assembly:
See evidence field. Verification anchors:
- Uncommit/commit split: unpatched 0x1C0066EF8 / patched 0x1C0066EB8 (test byte [rdi+0x38], 8).
- Commit-branch increment (both builds): unpatched 0x1C0066F34 / patched 0x1C0066F0D.
- Staged uncommit-branch increment (patched only): 0x1C0066EDC, feature checks at 0x1C0066ECA and 0x1C0066ED3.
- Join-point object uses: RecordVaPagingHistoryMapGpuVa unpatched 0x1C0066FB4 / patched 0x1C0066F94; ReleaseVaRangeReferenceSafe unpatched 0x1C0066FBF / patched 0x1C0066F9F.
- ReleaseVaRangeReference (0x1C0001338): 0x1C0001341 lock xadd [rcx+0x80], ebx (ebx=-1); free branch at 0x1C000135C.

breakpoints.why: Entry to the function. The Flags byte at [arg+0x38] bit 3 selects the uncommit branch. In the patched build, break at 0x1C0066ECA/0x1C0066ED3 to observe the feature-flag checks that gate the added increment; when either returns zero, control falls through to 0x1C0066EE3 (the unpatched path).

Feature_3895685435 gate guarding the AddVaRangeToVadRangeList + CleanupVaRangeReference block that drops a reference. Present identically in both builds. When the feature is disabled this block is skipped and no reference is dropped on the uncommit path.
Atomic refcount decrement (lock xadd at 0x1C0001341). The deleting destructor at 0x1C000135C runs only when the pre-decrement refcount was 1. Identical in both builds.

watch: dword [obj+0x40] & 0x2000 - managed-VAD flag; guards the reference-drop block in UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand
dword [obj+0x80] - reference count; incremented by 'lock inc' and decremented by ReleaseVaRangeReference's 'lock xadd'
Return values of Feature_3895685435__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage and Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage - determine whether the staged code paths execute at all
key_offsets: unpatched 0x1C0066EF8 / patched 0x1C0066EB8: test byte [rdi+0x38], 8 - uncommit vs commit split
unpatched 0x1C0066F34 / patched 0x1C0066F0D: lock inc dword [<obj>+0x80] - commit-branch increment (both builds)
patched 0x1C0066EDC: lock inc dword [rsi+0x80] - staged uncommit-branch increment (gated at 0x1C0066ECA and 0x1C0066ED3)
unpatched 0x1C0064536 / patched 0x1C0062E5A: Feature_3895685435 gate on the reference-drop block (both builds)
patched 0x1C0062EB7: compensating ReleaseVaRangeReference on the AddVaRangeToVadRangeList failure path (gated at 0x1C0062EA7)
0x1C0001341: lock xadd [rcx+0x80], ebx - refcount decrement in ReleaseVaRangeReference
0x1C000135C: call deleting destructor when pre-decrement refcount was 1
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Call D3DKMTMapGpuVirtualAddress with D3DDDI_MAPGPUVIRTUALADDRESS.Flags bit 3 (0x8) set to drive VidMmMapGpuVirtualAddressInternal into the uncommit branch. To observe any divergence between the two builds, the internal Feature_3895685435 feature must be enabled (so the uncommit path drops a reference) and the VA range must carry the managed-VAD flag 0x2000. In the default configuration these preconditions are not met and both binaries behave identically.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the default configuration (servicing flags disabled) the uncommit path does not drop a reference on the VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE object; the object remains live through RecordVaPagingHistoryMapGpuVa and ReleaseVaRangeReferenceSafe. No bugcheck, no freed-pool access. The patched and unpatched code paths are equivalent.
debugging_aids.notes: Verified struct offsets for VIDMM_MAPPED_VA_RANGE: +0x38 = request flags (bit 3 selects uncommit), +0x40 = flags field (bit 0x2000 = managed VAD, guards the reference-drop block), +0x80 = reference count (uint32). Other structure offsets were not verified and are not asserted here.

summary: The patch stages a reference-count-balancing change in dxgmms2.sys but does not deliver an unconditionally active security fix. In VidMmMapGpuVirtualAddressInternal the uncommit branch (D3DDDI_MAPGPUVIRTUALADDRESS.Flags bit 3 set) gains a 'lock inc dword [obj+0x80]' at 0x1C0066EDC, gated behind Feature_3895685435 and Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference; the unpatched no-increment path is retained as the fall-through. UncommitVirtualAddressRangeSystemCommand gains one compensating ReleaseVaRangeReference on the AddVaRangeToVadRangeList failure path under Feature_Servicing_VARangeHoldReference. The reference-drop these balance (CleanupVaRangeReference -> ReleaseVaRangeReference, guarded by Feature_3895685435 and the managed-VAD flag 0x2000) is present identically in both builds. With the servicing flags in their default disabled state the patched and unpatched binaries behave identically on this path and no use-after-free is reachable. The third changed function is an unrelated destructor-extraction refactor. Final assessment: no delivered, reachable security change (severity none).
